Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The Rotherham child sexual exploitation scandal consists of the organised child sexual abuse that occurred in the town of Rotherham, South Yorkshire, Northern England from the late 1980s until the 2010s and the failure of local authorities to act on reports of the abuse throughout most of that period. Researcher Angie Heal, who was hired by local officials and warned them about child exploitation occurring between 2002 and 2007, has since described it as the "biggest child protection scandal in UK history". Evidence of the abuse was first noted in the early 1990s, when care home managers investigated reports that children in their care were being picked up by taxi drivers. From at least 2001, multiple reports passed names of alleged perpetrators, several from one family, to the
B. The organized child sexual abuse, which took place in Rotherham, South Yorkshire, Northern England, starting in the late 1980s and continuing into the 2010s, along with the consistent inaction of local authorities regarding reports of this abuse for the majority of that timeframe, constitutes the Rotherham child sexual exploitation scandal. Angie Heal, a researcher engaged by local officials who subsequently alerted them to the child exploitation happening from 2002 to 2007, has since characterized this incident as "the biggest child protection scandal in UK history." The initial indications of this abuse surfaced in the early 1990s, when investigations were launched by care home managers into allegations that taxi drivers were collecting children under their supervision. Beginning at least in 2001, numerous reports were submitted, identifying alleged perpetrators, with several individuals belonging to a single family.
C. The Rotherham child sexual exploitation scandal encompasses the systematic abuse of children in Rotherham, South Yorkshire, Northern England from the late 1980s to the 2010s, and the failure of local authorities to act on reports of abuse.
